assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 7-13: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14. All four data points correctly extracted.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 29-39, section 'Ecosystem Detection': 'The vulnerable library quinn-proto is a Rust crate. Based on the Ecosystem Mappings table in the security matrix, this falls under the **Cargo** ecosystem' with Lock file: Cargo.lock and Check command: git show <tag>:Cargo.lock.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 27-33 lists all 7 versions from the supportability matrix: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (2.1.x stream) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (2.2.x stream). The security-matrix-mock.md defines exactly these 7 versions across both streams. No versions are skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 6-20 'Aggregated Supportability Matrix' maps each version to its pinned backend tag (2.1.0->v0.3.8, 2.1.1->v0.3.12, 2.2.0->v0.4.5, 2.2.1->v0.4.8, 2.2.2->v0.4.8 retag, 2.2.3->v0.4.11, 2.2.4->v0.4.12). The version impact table on lines 27-33 uses quinn-proto versions extracted from those specific tags. The Upstream Fix Status section (lines 48-55) explicitly distinguishes '(needs verification)' for branch HEAD from the tag-based data used in the impact table.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 32: '2.2.2 | 2.2.x | -- | YES | retag of 2.2.1 (same as 2.2.1)'. The retag is explicitly identified, the quinn-proto column shows '--' (no independent lock file), and the affected status YES is carried forward from 2.2.1.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 22-28: 'The PSIRT-assigned Affects Version "RHTPA 2.0.0" is incorrect' followed by diff on lines 25-26: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.0.0]' / 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. Each version change is individually rationalized on lines 30-35 with lock-file evidence.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names throughout: 'RHTPA 2.2.0', 'RHTPA 2.2.1', 'RHTPA 2.2.2' in both the proposed correction (line 26) and rationale (lines 30-35). The Proposed Jira Mutation section (lines 48-56) uses placeholder IDs '<jira-id-for-RHTPA-2.2.0>' with explicit note on line 58: 'Jira version IDs would be discovered dynamically via getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields in a live triage. The IDs shown above are placeholders.' No hardcoded Jira version IDs are present.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 (lines 33-77): Repository = 'backend' (line 37), Target Branch = 'release/0.4.z' (line 39), Acceptance Criteria checklist (lines 66-68), Dependencies = 'Depends on: TC-8001' (line 76). Task 2 (lines 79-123): Repository = 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 89), Target Branch = 'main' (line 93), Acceptance Criteria (lines 112-113), Dependencies include 'Depends on: [upstream-task-key] (upstream backport must merge first)' (line 120) and 'Depends on: TC-8001' (line 121). Jira Linkage section (lines 125-132) specifies Blocks dependency between downstream and upstream.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 23: 'Since quinn-proto is a Cargo (source dependency) ecosystem, **two tasks** are created for this stream.' Task 1 is 'Upstream Backport Task (2.2.x)' (line 25) and Task 2 is 'Downstream Propagation Subtask (2.2.x)' (line 79). Jira Linkage step 3 (line 129): 'Link downstream task as blocked by upstream task with "Blocks"'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses 'Proposed Correction' (line 22), 'Proposed Jira Mutation' (line 48), and 'Proposed Comment' (line 62) framing throughout. remediation.md line 256: 'The following comment would be posted to TC-8001' (conditional framing). Jira Linkage sections (lines 125-132, 249-251) describe actions that 'would' be performed. Post-Triage Summary (lines 273-282) uses descriptive bullet points for proposed actions rather than asserting they were executed. No output file claims mutations were actually performed.
